_comment: Reviewed Layer-4 collision-matrix baseline for the npm-package tool registry (Novada MCP). Consumed by tests/tools/collision-matrix.test.ts. Originally promoted from reports/phase1-net-design/B/prototype/collision-matrix.mjs. RE-BASELINED (round-1 red-team hardening pass): paramShape() now recurses into array items / anyOf-oneOf-allOf members / nested object properties / const / required instead of collapsing them to a bare {type}, so this file's shape data must be the ACTUAL live JSON-schema fragment for each side, not a hand-summarized type+enum. RE-BASELINED AGAIN (round-2 red-team pass, 2026-07-19): paramShape() now recurses the FULL JSON-Schema 2020-12 Applicator vocabulary (additionalProperties/propertyNames/patternProperties/prefixItems added on top of round 1's set) and sorts anyOf/oneOf/allOf members so a cosmetic reorder no longer busts the match — see collision-matrix.test.ts's header comment for the exact GAP D/E/F fixes. Re-running the live registry through the round-2 normalizer surfaced exactly ONE stale entry: the novada_browser vs novada_browser_flow 'actions' collision below, whose recorded shapes were missing `additionalProperties:false` (present on every real zod-emitted object schema) — updated to match the live schema exactly; the underlying divergence and its accept-as-is rationale are UNCHANGED, only the literal shape fragment was corrected for round-2 completeness. No other entry in this file changed and no new, previously-invisible collision was surfaced by the round-2 fix (additionalProperties/propertyNames/patternProperties/prefixItems only affect nested-object-valued params in this registry's actual schemas — novada_scrape's params record and novada_browser's/novada_browser_flow's actions union — and only the latter pair collides on a shared param name). Do NOT add or edit an entry here without a human reviewer confirming the divergence is intentional and adequately disambiguated by the two tools' descriptions — adding to this file silences a real future warning. Every 'shapes'/'shape' field is re-hashed at test-load time via the SAME recursive paramShape() the live scan uses, so ANY drift in the real schema (enum grows, nesting changes, etc.) busts the match and forces re-review — this file is not just documentation, it is re-verified byte-for-byte every run.

expectedSharedParams.name: url
expectedSharedParams.note: Conceptually one shared idea ("the target URL") but NOT one single shape: novada_extract additionally accepts an array of URLs for batch mode (its own 'urls' alias plus this anyOf). Two reviewed variants recorded below — a pair is only skipped when EACH side's live shape matches one of these two exactly. A third, undocumented shape for 'url' anywhere in the registry will NOT be silently exempted; it will surface as a new, unreviewed collision.

expectedSharedParams.name: urls
expectedSharedParams.note: Only novada_extract declares this alias param today (single variant). Pinned so if a second tool ever adds 'urls' with a different item shape it surfaces for review instead of silently riding an exemption.

expectedSharedParams.name: format
expectedSharedParams.note: The word 'format' is shared vocabulary but each tool's format param is a genuinely different, tool-specific output-format vocabulary by design (search/crawl/monitor/session_stats/search_feedback share one small json|markdown vocabulary; extract/scrape/proxy/account each define their own richer or orthogonal enum — proxy's 'format' isn't even a content format, it's the shape of a credential string). Five reviewed variants recorded below. Any 'format' shape NOT matching one of these five exactly (e.g. a 6th tool inventing a new enum) is NOT exempted and must be reviewed.

expectedSharedParams.name: project
expectedSharedParams.note: Uniform across every tool that declares it (single reviewed variant): a plain optional string for subfolder grouping.

expectedSharedParams.name: country
expectedSharedParams.note: Uniform across every tool that declares it (single reviewed variant): a plain string ISO country code.

expectedSharedParams.name: session_id
expectedSharedParams.note: Uniform across every tool that declares it (single reviewed variant): a plain string session token.

expectedSharedParams.name: query
expectedSharedParams.note: Uniform across every tool that declares it (single reviewed variant): a plain non-empty string.

expectedSharedParams.name: limit
expectedSharedParams.note: Uniform across every tool that declares it (single reviewed variant): a plain integer.

expectedSharedParams.name: page
expectedSharedParams.note: Uniform across every tool that declares it (single reviewed variant): a plain integer.

expectedSharedParams.name: confirm
expectedSharedParams.note: Uniform across every WRITE tool that declares it (single reviewed variant): a literal boolean const:true two-step confirm gate.

expectedSharedParams.name: params
expectedSharedParams.note: Only novada_scrape declares this today (single variant): a free-form object of operation-specific parameters.

expectedSharedParams.name: id
expectedSharedParams.note: Only novada_ip_whitelist declares this today (single variant): a plain string entry ID.

expectedSharedParams.name: products
expectedSharedParams.note: Only novada_account declares this today (single variant): an array of product-name strings.

acceptedDivergentCollisions.note: Same escalation concept (auto -> static -> render), narrower enum on crawl. novada_extract's render enum additionally includes the 'browser'/'js' aliases; novada_crawl only supports auto/static/render.
Same divergence as extract vs crawl: novada_site_copy's render enum is the narrower auto/static/render subset, missing extract's browser/js aliases.

Different code-spaces for the same word: proxy_account_create's product spans the full developer-api product catalog (1=Residential,2=Rotating ISP,3=Rotating Datacenter,4=Unlimited,7=Unblocker,9=Mobile). ip_whitelist's product is restricted to the 3 products that support IP whitelisting (1=Residential,4=Unlimited,5=Static ISP). Both tools' descriptions spell out the numeric meaning inline, so the divergence is disambiguated for an agent reading either schema alone.

novada_proxy_account_list shares novada_proxy_account_create's product enum (same developer-api product codes) — same divergence against ip_whitelist's narrower 1/4/5 set as the create-vs-whitelist entry above.

Different concepts sharing a name, both now enums with disjoint value-spaces: proxy_account_create's status is a closed enum ('1'=active|'-3'=disabled) for account state. static_ip_mgmt's status (NOV-853 hardening, 2026-07-19: widened from a bare z.string() to z.enum(["","1","2","3"]) so an out-of-domain filter value is rejected instead of silently passed through) is a closed enum for an IP-status filter (''=All, '1'=In use, '2'=Expired, '3'=Released per its description). Different shapes are correct: one is account state, the other is an IP-status filter — divergence is intentional, just no longer one side being unconstrained.

novada_proxy_account_list shares novada_proxy_account_create's status enum — same divergence against static_ip_mgmt's now-enum status filter (see create-vs-static_ip_mgmt entry above for the NOV-853 rationale).

Disjoint verb sets for unrelated domains: ip_whitelist's action is add/list/del/remark (whitelist CRUD). capture_apikey's action is get/reset (key rotation). Same param name, no overlapping values, each tool's own enum + description disambiguates the meaning.
ip_whitelist's action is add/list/del/remark (whitelist CRUD). static_ip_mgmt's action is open/renew/export/list (IP-lease lifecycle). Only 'list' overlaps in meaning; the rest diverge by design.
capture_apikey's action is get/reset (key rotation). static_ip_mgmt's action is open/renew/export/list (IP-lease lifecycle). Same param name, fully disjoint enums, disjoint domains.

RED-TEAM GAP A FIX — newly surfaced only after paramShape() started recursing into array items/oneOf members/nested properties; previously both sides hashed to the same bare {"type":"array"} and this collision was completely invisible (never reviewed). Reviewed and ACCEPTED: novada_browser's 'actions' is a 14-variant discriminated union keyed on an 'action' const (navigate/click/type/screenshot/snapshot/aria_snapshot/evaluate/wait/scroll/hover/press_key/select/close_session/list_sessions) for the full-CDP tool. novada_browser_flow's 'actions' is a flat object with a 5-value 'type' enum (click/scroll/wait/type/screenshot) for the simplified-flow tool — a deliberately smaller, differently-shaped vocabulary (different discriminator field name too: 'action' vs 'type'). Each tool's own description explicitly cross-references the other ('novada_browser is the primary, more capable tool... Prefer novada_browser for all new browser automation' / 'Use novada_browser_flow when: you specifically need the simplified flow API'), so an agent reading either schema is pointed at the distinction. Keep as-is — no runtime change warranted; surfacing this in Layer 4 was the point of the red-team fix, not a signal to merge or rename either tool. ROUND-2 UPDATE (2026-07-19): shapes[] corrected to include additionalProperties:false on every object member (present in the real live schema, previously omitted from this recorded fragment since round-1 paramShape never walked additionalProperties at all) — this is a literal-accuracy fix only, not a re-review of the underlying divergence, which is unchanged and remains ACCEPTED.
